Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user has given me a loan application process with strict normative rules and some recommended best practices I need to analyze three cases from an event log to see where they violate the rules or fall short of recommendations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I should clearly separate the normative violations which are serious from the desiderata issues which are just recommended but not required The normative rules are absolute skipping steps wrong order or doing forbidden actions like finalizing documentation after rejection are all big no-nos The desiderata are about timing and optional steps like follow-up calls which arent mandatory

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at Case 1 Sequence Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Approved Inform Applicant Finalize Documentation Close Case This follows the exact normative sequence perfectly All steps are in order no skips no forbidden actions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: For desiderata Credit check was within 24 hours 9am to 930am same day but inform applicant was at 1pm which is 2 hours after decision well within 48 hours No follow-up call mentioned but since its not required thats fine So Case 1 is clean

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 is messy First issue Risk Assessment at 820am but Credit Check only at 900am That violates the normative rule that Credit Check must come before Risk Assessment Big problem Then Loan Decision Rejected at 930am which is fine since its after Risk Assessment even though Risk Assessment was out of order But then Finalize Documentation at 1000am after rejection Thats explicitly forbidden by normative rules do not finalize documentation after a rejection

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 Sequence looks correct Application Credit Check but 2 days late which is just desiderata Risk Assessment Loan Decision Approved Inform Applicant 2 days late Finalize Documentation Close Case Normative violations None all steps in perfect order no forbidden actions

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Desiderata issues Credit check took 53 hours from 9am Jan 12 to 10am Jan 14 violates within 24 hours recommendation Informing applicant took 51 hours from 12pm Jan 14 to 3pm Jan 16 violates within 48 hours recommendation No follow-up call before closing violates the recommended best practice

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I should structure my response clearly 1 First list normative violations per case with specific rule breaks 2 Then list desiderata misses per case 3 For each explain why it matters and whether its serious

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The task asks to suggest interpretations or justifications where possible For Case 3s timing delays maybe system issues or workload But the normative rules dont care about reasons the violations are still violations regardless of why they happened So I should state that while timing delays are suboptimal they dont violate hard rules
